He holds a world record for reeling off 300 punches in 30 seconds, all while holding 1kg dumbbells in each hand.

Other records to his name through Official Record Breakers involve performing six martial arts kicking techniques in less than five-and-a-half seconds and a number involving pulling or lifting vehicles.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

He added: “I’m always trying to challenge and push the boundaries of what I can and can’t do physically.



“Everything I do, I do it as a legacy for my children so they can look back once I’ve gone and see what sort of man I was.”

News.title: Dubai new high rise hit by Iran drone - footage shows hole punched in building
News.content:
Dubai new high rise hit by Iran drone - footage shows hole punched in building

Shocking footage caught a fire burning high up a luxe apartment building in Dubai in the early hours of Thursday morning

Fire rages after Iranian drone hits tower block in Dubai

Another luxury high rise has been hit by Iranian drones as shocking footage showed a fire raging at a skyscraper.

‌



A terrifying video caught the aftermath of another retaliatory attack on Dubai as this strike punctured a hole into the glitzy block. The blaze ripped through a building near the Creek Harbour neighbourhood of the state, around 10km away from the international airport. Emergency services rushed to the scene in a desperate effort to put out the fire, which raged in the middle of the night.

‌



Horror images and video showed a thick cloud of smoke billowing from the apartment block in the early hours of Thursday morning while a section glowed from the inferno.

‌



View 3 Images A drone hit a luxury building in Dubai (Image: X)

World Cup disruption or cancellation fears as US-Iran tensions escalate READ MORE :

The authorities have so far not reported any causalities from the latest attack and said all residents were "safe". This comes after Dubai International Airport was hit again on Wednesday.

Four people - Ghanaian nationals, one Indian national and one Bangladeshi citizen - were left injured after the Iranian drone attack on one of the world's busiest airports. Local authorities confirmed that two drones were spotted at the transport hub.

‌



View 3 Images The attack left a massive hole in the building (Image: AFP via Getty Images)

In a post on X, Dubai officials wrote: "Authorities confirm that two drones fell in the vicinity of Dubai International Airport (DXB) a short while ago, resulting in minor injuries to two Ghanaian nationals and one Bangladeshi national, and moderate injuries to one Indian national. Air traffic is operating as normal."

In a statement, the UAE Ministry of Defence said: "UAE air defences are currently responding to incoming missile and drone threats from Iran. MOD asserts that the sounds heard are the result of the Air Defence Systems intercepting missiles and drones. The public are requested to adhere to the safety and security instructions issued."

‌



View 3 Images Workers inspecting the damage on Thursday (Image: AP)

A passenger inside the airport at the time, speaking to the BBC, said people inside were told to move away from glass windows and take shelter in a more protected area.

This comes as a British tourist who allegedly filmed a missile flying over the popular Emirate state, while on holiday, faced jail. A 60-year-old man from London was accused of breaking the UAE' strict laws against filming or uploading social media posts of conflict, according to legal advocacy group Detained in Dubai.

Article continues below

The Brit was reportedly arrested on Monday and is one of 21 people are facing the same fate and up to 2 years in prison.

Radha Stirling, director of the London-based Detained in Dubai organisation, said: "Amongst those charged is a British man who says he deleted the video immediately when asked and meant no harm, but is still among those facing charges."

News.title: Man found hanged in Winter Wonderland was due in court over 'indecent images'
News.content:
Man found hanged in Winter Wonderland was due in court over 'indecent images'

WARNING - DISTRESSING CONTENT: Kyle Samuel, 33, from Stoke-on-Trent, died shortly after being found at the Winter Wonderland ice rink where he worked

View Image Winter Wonderland Stoke (Image: Pete Stonier / Stoke Sentinel)

A man was found hanging at a Winter Wonderland ice rink.

‌



‌



Kyle Samuel, 29, was found at Winter Wonderland Stoke in Hanley, Stoke-on-Trent, at 9am on November 11. He was taken to the Royal Stoke University Hospital, where he died later that morning, an inquest heard. Mr Samuel, who lived and worked at the attraction, was due in court that day, charged with making indecent images of children, Stoke-on-Trent Live reports.

‌



Coroner Daniel Howe recorded a suicide verdict, stating Mr Samuel passed away due to hypoxia caused by hanging. The coroner added: "He had a history of low mood and was previously talking about ending his own life. The recent trigger was impending court proceedings due to take place that day.

"Kyle attempted to contact friends and family members the evening before he was found deceased. From the evidence I’ve heard I’m satisfied that Kyle intended to hang himself and end his life, as he had expressed recent stress and worry for the upcoming court proceedings."

‌



Ensure our latest headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as your Preferred Source in your Google search settings.

In a witness statement, Kyle’s mother said: "Kyle rang me on November 10. He was going to leave Hanley at 7am as the drive was around one-and-a-half hours so I should expect him at around 8.30am. He seemed in good spirits and he ended the phone call saying he loved me but that was not unusual as he always did this.

"But I became aware that he’d also tried to call his nan that night as well as some friends. I believe that was because he was planning to end his own life the following day.

Article continues below

"Kyle was devoted and dedicated to his career which was his passion. He loved the circus industry from a young age and he performed as a clown for years at a circus and he adored his job. However, this showed him he could put on a facade for others and not let anyone know how he truly felt about his mental health.

"He was the most loveable, kind, caring, gentle giant ever known. He was all I could ask for in a son and I’m grateful to call him my son. He will be deeply missed by everyone who ever loved him.”

For emotional support you can call the Samaritans 24-hour helpline on 116 123, email jo@samaritans.org, visit a Samaritans branch in person or go to the Samaritans website.

News.title: RAF Lossiemouth LIVE as Moray homes 'evacuated' over suspicious package
News.content:
A suspicious package has been found at one of the UK's largest RAF bases.

Bomb squads have been dispatched to RAF Lossiemouth in Moray, and a cordon is in place around the Muirton Road area. It is understood that some residents in the area have been evacuated from their homes, the Press and Journal reports.

The RAF said it is "working with civilian agencies" to resolve the issue.

News.title: Kate Middleton gives health update and reveals what she's cut back since cancer diagnosis
News.content:
Kate Middleton gives health update and reveals what she's cut back since cancer diagnosis

The Prince and Princess of Wales made a surprise visit to the Bermondsey Beer Mile in London today, where they poured pints - and Kate made a rare reference to her cancer diagnosis

Prince William and Princess Kate pour pints on the Bermondsey Beer Mile

The Princess of Wales left the beer tasting to her husband on a visit to a brewery today when she said that she had cut back on alcohol "since my diagnosis". Kate, 44, had chemotherapy in 2024 and last year announced that she was in remission from cancer.

‌



During a visit to Fabal beerhall, Kate and Prince William were told about the Bermondsey Beer Mile, which is home to several craft breweries and tap rooms. When she was invited to try some beer and cider, Kate told owner Hannah Rhodes: “Since my diagnosis I haven’t had much alcohol.” She added: “It’s something I have to be a lot more conscious of now.” Rhodes, who offered Kate a soft drink instead, said: “Fair enough.”

‌



Kate Middleton stuns shoppers on surprise market visit with William to Tik Tok sensations READ MORE :

Kate Middleton handed huge role at major royal event that's just days away READ MORE :

View 4 Images Willliam and Kate visit the Fabal Beerhall on the Bermondsey Beer Mile today (Image: AP)

‌



Turning to Prince William, Kate patted her husband’s knee and said: “But you like your cider, don’t you?” William then tried a fleet of beers and ciders, some of which came from Rhodes’ brand Hiver, which uses honey from bee hives as a replacement for sugar in the fermentation process.

Kate, who is known to be a beekeeper with hives at her Norfolk home of Anmer Hall, tried some of the honey by dipping a stick into a jar. The princess said: “If you take the honey off the hive in early summer it tastes very different.”

Speaking about harvesting honey from the hives, she added: “If you take the honey off when it’s wet it is a natural fermentation process anyway." William laughed and joked: “She knows a lot about bees. Be careful what you say, you might be corrected.”

‌



View 4 Images The couple try honey, which is used to make beer (Image: AP)

Earlier William and Kate tried their hand at brewing at nearby Southwark Brewing company, a craft beer company further down the Bermondsey Beer Mile which was founded in 2014 as one of the area’s first small-batch breweries under the railway arches.

Greeting Peter Jackson, 66, they were shown how to ascend a ladder where William wore black rubber gloves to add hops to the “kettle”. After tipping the hops in, William said: “It smells like horse feed as you’re doing it. There’s a real mixture of smells.” Jackson joked: “Yes. It’s good that it changes by the time it gets to your glass.” Kate then went up the ladder to stir them in.

‌



They then had a competition to see who could pull the best pint, which was declared to be better than the owner. Peter Jackson told them: “Do you know what? You can come back on Saturday for a shift.”

View 4 Images The couple have a go at pulling beers at the Southwark Brewing Company on the Bermondsey Beer Mile (Image: PA)

He then pulled out his mobile phone to make a video call to his son, Will Jackson, an accountant who was celebrating his 34th birthday in Manchester. After showing Jackson how to start the call on his phone, the prince said: “William, happy birthday. I hope you’ve got a good party tonight.”

‌



When told he was off to a restaurant to celebrate, the prince said: “Is your dad paying? We’re just in here trying not to ruin his brewery. We’ve just been given a pint-pulling contest.” William later said: “Pubs are so important,” saying that they were “Places for people and the community to come together.”

After leaving the breweries, the couple then travelled upstream by boat to the RNLI lifeboat station on the Thames. At the RNLI Tower Lifeboat Station, the Prince and Princess will hear about the work of the crews and volunteers saving lives along the Thames.

View 4 Images Kate stirs a brewhouse kettle during a visit to the Southwark Brewing Company (Image: PA)

‌



They will meet the duty crew, hear about recent rescues, and speak to lifeguards who are currently preparing for the upcoming Boat Race.

Tower is the RNLI’s busiest lifeboat station, operating 24 hours a day, 365 days a year. This visit comes as the RNLI marks the 25th anniversary of RNLI Lifeguards, who also patrol Britain’s beaches and support major river events. The Prince last visited the previous Tower Station site in 2016 and has also met RNLI crews in Cornwall and Anglesey.

Article continues below

News.title: O2 customers given free network upgrade with faster speeds from today
News.content:
O2 customers given free network upgrade with faster speeds from today

O2 has started rolling out a complimentary 5G+ network upgrade to all Pay Monthly customers, but you'll need a compatible phone and SIM card to access the faster speeds

View 2 Images The prevention quiz is free to complete online (stock photo) (Image: Getty)

Mobile network giant O2 has revealed it's rolling out access to a superior tier of 5G connectivity for all its Pay Monthly subscribers - provided they've got a compatible handset.

‌



The telecoms firm is currently enhancing its nationwide network with 5G standalone technology (which it markets as 5G+), a variant of 5G signal that operates independently from legacy 4G infrastructure, and is consequently touted to deliver swifter, more dependable connections.

‌



O2 further suggests subscribers might notice enhanced battery performance, as handsets won't be required to juggle connections between 4G and 5G networks at once, with 5G+ speeds pledged to be "around 50 percent faster" than 4G.

‌



John Lewis to pay first staff bonus in four years to thousands of workers READ MORE :

View 2 Images O2 is rolling out improved 5G nationwide. (Image: Getty/Samsung)

The network said 5G+ is now live in over 700 towns and cities nationwide, with all Pay Monthly customers now receiving this enhancement without any additional charge to their current tariffs.

To capitalise on this complimentary benefit, you'll need a handset that supports 5G+ technology. Fortunately, the roster of eligible devices is fairly extensive, with every iPhone launched from 2024's iPhone 15 onwards capable of tapping into 5G+ signals, reports the Express.

‌



Most well-known Samsung Galaxy handsets stretching back to 2021 are likewise supported, as are Google Pixel devices from the past two years.

"Our 5G+ service is now available to millions of customers across the UK, and uniquely, it's available to everyone on O2 across all Plans - unlike other networks," said Christian Hindennach, Chief Commercial Officer at Virgin Media O2.

"The upgrade boosts performance in busy areas, including major venues that host big crowds, benefiting both residents and visitors, and is another example of Virgin Media O2's commitment to enhancing our network and delivering a more reliable experience."

‌



Competitor EE provides 5G standalone technology, though exclusively for those subscribed to particular premium pay monthly packages. You can check whether your location has O2 5G+ coverage using this online checker tool.

However, O2 has clarified that possessing a 5G+ compatible handset alone isn't sufficient - you'll also require an appropriate SIM card, which must either be a physical card purchased from 2024 onwards or an eSIM to function correctly with this cutting-edge technology.

"Older SIM cards, including those issued before 2024, were only designed to support earlier generations of mobile technology," an O2 spokesperson explained to Express.co.uk..

‌



"To access newer capabilities like 5G+, customers need a newer R15 SIM, which includes updated features that enable the network and device to use 5G Standalone services. O2 began issuing those to all new customers from 2024 onwards, and any customer who wants can upgrade to one now free of charge if they want 5G+."

According to O2, should you need to swap your SIM to gain access to 5G+, this can be arranged without any additional charge through the company's website.

eSIM represents fairly recent technology enabling mobile phones to register virtual SIMs, eliminating the need for the conventional plastic card that's traditionally slotted into devices. The drive to encourage punters towards eSIM technology stems partly from enabling access to cutting-edge innovations like 5G+, though it may also be laying the groundwork for smartphones eventually ditching physical SIM cards altogether.

Article continues below

Every iPhone sold stateside now operates exclusively on eSIM, meaning they're devoid of traditional SIM card slots - a shift that hasn't yet materialised in Britain, but could well be on the horizon. The iPhone Air launched in September 2025 is an eSIM-only device, even in the UK.

Whilst eSIMs can be transferred electronically between gadgets, users sacrifice the convenience of simply removing a physical SIM card when switching to a different handset.

News.title: Kids rooms will 'stay nice and tidy' with £20 B&M 'stylish' wall storage unit
News.content:
Kids rooms will 'stay nice and tidy' with £20 B&M 'stylish' wall storage unit

The shelf is 'great for keeping books, toys and teddies organised'

View 3 Images B&M has the affordable solution on offer for £20 (Image: © Chris Ratcliffe/Bloomberg via Getty Images)

Parents who want to bring some order to their lives can keep their children's favourite toys and items in a neat, organised place with an affordable B&M solution. This storage takes up no floor space and makes the most of underused storage potential.

‌



The Kids Home Four-Tier Shelf attaches to the wall and keeps books, toys, and more off the floor, leaving surface space free. It maximises the amount of storage for loose items that children may want quick access to.

‌



The wall-mounted shelf will need some assembly from its flat-pack. Once ready, it can be mounted on a child's bedroom wall for easy, accessible storage.

‌



A B&M spokesperson said: "This stylish and functional Kids Home Four-Tier Shelf is the perfect piece of decorative storage for a child's room. Great for keeping books, toys and teddies organised."

There are similar storage solutions like this available at other homeware stores like Dunelm. The Amber Wall Mounted Bookshelf (currently £28, down from £35) comes in five different colour options and has the same amount of storage space.

View 3 Images The Kids Home Four-Tier Shelf (Image: B&M)

‌



The Dunelm version offers a way to " keep children's rooms tidy". A spokesperson said: "This stunning wall-mounted bookshelf will add a touch of style to your child's home whilst providing a neat space to store their books, toys, and treasures."

Both options use wooden rods to keep items like books and toys secure on the shelves and not at risk of falling off. In terms of size, these two pieces of furniture are very similar.

The B&M unit measures out at 120cm tall with shelves that all span 50cm and stick out by 15cm. At Dunelm, the four-tier shelves are shorter at 112cm tall, but they do offer a slight bit of extra space on the shelves themselves, with nearly 60cm of space across, but sticking out at a more compact 12cm deep.

‌



View 3 Images Amber Wall Mounted Book Shelf (Image: Dunelm)

Depending on the room's layout and what children need to display on the shelves, either option could work for families in need of extra storage. People can see the full range of kids' furniture from B&M here.

In other B&M news, the furniture options go beyond the bedroom. Many shoppers are impressed by bargain options for the living room, kitchen, bathroom, and garden.

Article continues below

Recently, the discount store offered something that gamers would appreciate - and it cost a fraction of the price of something more expensive. The ‘sophisticated’ £70 gaming chair looked remarkably similar to an Amazon version that was priced at £360.

News.title: Lawyer says you'll know if someone's lying with 'talk about this later' technique
News.content:
Lawyer says you'll know if someone's lying with 'talk about this later' technique

Law firm owner and communications expert Jefferson Fisher shared a trick for catching liars

View Image People might be able to catch liars using the lawyer's tip (stock photo) (Image: Getty)

A lawyer has shared 'how to catch a liar in action' by paying attention during conversations. Jefferson Fisher is a US-based law firm owner and communications expert. He is an author, podcast host, and content creator, with over 1.5m followers on his popular TikTok account @Justaskjefferson.

‌



In his videos, Jefferson regularly shares tips for improving relationships and friendships, drawing on his legal career to offer advice. In a recent post, he shared a video captioned: "This is how to catch a liar in action."

‌



Jefferson told viewers: "There is nothing a liar hates more than unresolvedness." Explaining the mindset of liars, he said: "They want you to buy the lie now. Now they want it in a bow and a package. And you go, yes, I have it. They want it gone, off the plate. They want you to buy it immediately.

‌



"And when you don't, when you go, 'You know what, something just doesn't feel right. Something feels off. We can bring this up later.' People who tell the truth go, 'Okay, no problem.' You don't need an excuse for the truth.

"But the people who are not telling the truth will go, 'What do you think? You think I'm not telling the truth? What are you calling me a liar?' And they pump it up. People who tell the truth, they're totally good with you."

Content cannot be displayed without consent

‌



He says people who are telling the truth will most likely reply with something like: "'Take your time. Yeah, I'm happy to tell you exactly what happened. You can talk to so and so and so and so.'" The lawyer added: "People who are liars, they don't like things being strung out. They need it now." The video impressed social media users, gaining thousands of likes.

In a different post, Jefferson explained 'how to know if someone is being fake with you.' He claimed: "If somebody's being fake with you, there are really three things that you gotta know."

He said: "Number one is what I call bestie bombing. What they're doing is, 'Oh, my gosh, we're literally the same person. I think we're best friends and we just met.'

‌



Content cannot be displayed without consent

"They give way too much right out of the gate of how much they love you. That's not what secure people do. Secure people don't attach to you instantly."

Jefferson went on: "Two is the over-compliments. We all have this sixth sense to be able to sniff out if that's real or not. Like, nobody needs to teach you if it's a fake laugh or not."

Article continues below

Thirdly, he said you have to watch out for people who aren't interested in you and only talk about themselves. Once more, the video proved popular, gaining over 30k likes.

Passengers are urged to keep up to date with Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport's social media channels while the issue is resolved.
